summary: Trump's threat to oust Fed Chair Powell — with his term expiring May 15 — has put central bank independence at the center of market attention this week. Meanwhile, the Iran ceasefire remains the dominant macro swing factor, with WTI crude now below $84 after trading above $112 just a month ago. The Strait of Hormuz remains closed as the ceasefire clock winds down, keeping energy and risk assets in a tug-of-war.
tweet: Fed independence under fire as Trump's May 15 Powell deadline looms, while the Strait of Hormuz ceasefire clock keeps crude and equities on edge.
